message.content:
Research the MANUFACTURING FACILITIES and CERTIFICATIONS of Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd, an Indian Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) company in Mysore.

Use WebSearch then WebFetch on authoritative sources (vinyasit.com, annual reports, screener.in, news).

Gather:
1. Manufacturing facility locations (Mysore plants, any others) and number of facilities.
2. Capacity (SMT lines, production area, etc.) if disclosed.
3. Certifications: AS9100, ISO 9001/13485/14001, defense-related approvals (e.g., from defense PSUs, NABL, environmental).

You have WebSearch and WebFetch tools. Run several searches and fetch the most authoritative pages.

Return a concise markdown bullet list of findings. After EACH fact put the exact source URL in parentheses. Distinguish confirmed facts from estimates. Flag anything unverified. Do NOT write files — return findings as your text output.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' Mysore manufacturing facilities and SMT lines:

## Manufacturing Facility

Vinyas Innovative Technologies' manufacturing facility is located in Mysore, India, with a total of 1,50,000 sq.ft. of production area.

## SMT Lines and Plant Capabilities

Print Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA) is the core competency of Vinyas, with the company utilizing high-speed SMT (Surface Mount Technology) lines and THT (Through-Hole Technology) to manufacture complex boards for precision instruments.

The facility handles processes ranging from complex and diminutive SMT assemblies to through-hole assembly with dedicated teams assigned to each line of specialization.

## Recent Expansion

Following its successful SME IPO in late 2023, Vinyas is aggressively expanding its manufacturing footprint, investing in new SMT lines and enhancing its R&D capabilities.

## Company Focus

Headquartered in Mysore, India, Vinyas is an Electronic Manufacturing Services (EMS) and Original Design Manufacturing (ODM) partner that specializes in providing end-to-end solutions for Electronic System Design and Manufacturing (ESDM), serving diverse high-reliability sectors including aerospace, defense, medical, automotive, and industrial electronics.

I found search results related to Vinyas Innovative Technologies. Here's what the search revealed:

## Company Overview

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited is an India-based integrated electronic manufacturing services provider. The company was incorporated in 2001 and focuses on designing, engineering, and electronics manufacturing services.

## Business Operations

The company provides end-to-end solutions including design for manufacturability, supply chain management, PCB assembly and advanced test solutions, product integration, testing and box build assemblies, and after-market support to OEMs and ODMs.

## Financial Performance

On a full-year basis, the company's net profit surged 26.5% to Rs 19.42 crore on a 25% rise in revenue to Rs 396.64 crore in FY25 over FY24.

## Recent Developments

As of 29 May 2026, the company incorporated wholly owned subsidiary Vinyas Innovative Technologies Inc. in the USA to support overseas expansion.

The search results show various financial sources (Tijori Finance, Investing.com, Screener.in, MarketScreener, TradingView) where you can access detailed annual report information and capacity details. An NSE annual report PDF was also found but could not be directly accessed through the search.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Vinyas Innovative Technologies' certifications:

## Key Certifications

Vinyas Innovative Technologies Limited (VITL) is an ISO 9001:2015, AS 9100-D and ISO 13485:2016 certified company involved in electronics manufacturing services. The company is also IATF certified.

## Sectoral Focus

Vinyas has been a trusted player in the Indian electronics manufacturing industry for over 23 years, catering to various industries such as Aerospace, Defence, Medical, Automotive, Industrial, and Telecommunications.

## Defense Expertise

The company is one of the few Indian EMS players qualified to handle sophisticated defense electronics, benefiting from strict "Offset" obligations and indigenization policies. The company secured AS9100 certification, allowing it to enter the aerospace and defense supply chains, marking the transition from being a vendor to becoming a strategic partner for major Indian defense PSUs.

Regarding NABL certification specifically, the search results do not contain information about that particular accreditation for Vinyas.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd - Manufacturing & Certifications

Based on the webpage content provided, here's what is disclosed about the company's manufacturing capabilities and certifications:

## Certifications

The company holds three key certifications:
- **ISO 9001:2015** (Quality Management)
- **AS 9100-D** (Aerospace standard)
- **ISO 13485:2016** (Medical devices standard)

## Manufacturing & Services

Vinyas provides "design, engineering, and electronics manufacturing services (EMS)" with capabilities including:
- Printed circuit board (PCB) design and assembly
- Design for manufacturability
- Supply chain management
- Advanced test solutions
- Product integration and testing
- Box build assemblies
- After-market support

## Notable Absence

**The webpage does not contain specific information about:**
- Number of manufacturing plants or facilities
- Physical plant locations
- Production area measurements or capacity details
- SMT (Surface Mount Technology) line counts
- Defense approvals or NABL certifications

The content focuses on service offerings and certifications rather than detailed facility infrastructure. For comprehensive facility information, you would need to consult their annual reports or investor presentations linked on the page.

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies - Certifications & Standards

Based on the webpage content, here are the documented certifications and quality standards:

## Organizational Certifications
- **ISO 9001:2015** - Quality management system certification

## Industry-Specific Certifications
- **AS 9100-D** - Aerospace and Defence standard
- **NADCAP** - National Aerospace and Defense Contractors Accreditation Program
- **Defence Industry License**
- **ISO 13485:2016** - Medical devices quality management system

## Manufacturing Process Standards
The company follows several IPC standards including:
- IPC-J-STD-003B (Baking)
- IPC-CM-770 (SMD and Through Hole Assembly)
- IPC-HDBK-001 (Forming, Reflow, Cleaning, Post Soldering)
- J-STD-001E (Wave Soldering, Post Soldering Operations)
- IPC-A-610D (PCBA acceptance for Classes 1, 2 & 3)
- IPC-WHMA-A-620A (Wire Harness acceptance for Classes 1, 2 & 3)

## Personnel Certifications
The organization maintains certified personnel including Six Sigma, IPC Standard certified, Joint Industry Standard certified, and ISO Internal Auditors across their team.

content.text:
I have enough to compile a well-sourced answer.

## Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd — Manufacturing Facilities & Certifications

### Manufacturing Facilities (Locations & Number)
- **CONFIRMED — Primary manufacturing facility in Mysuru (Mysore), Karnataka.** Address: KIADB Plot No. 19, Survey No. 26 & 273P, 3rd Phase, Koorgalli Industrial Area, Koorgalli, Mysuru-570018 (https://www.vinyasit.com/)
- **CONFIRMED — Main facility was inaugurated in 2014; company was originally established in 2001 with semi-auto SMT lines** (https://www.vinyasit.com/)
- **CONFIRMED — A dedicated export unit operates under the Electronic Hardware Technology Park (EHTP) scheme** (https://www.vinyasit.com/)
- **CONFIRMED — A dedicated Class III facility** (high-reliability/defense-grade assembly) is offered as a capability (https://www.indiamart.com/vinyasinnovative-technologies/)
- **UNVERIFIED — Exact number of separate "plants."** Company materials describe one primary world-class facility plus the EHTP export unit and a Class III facility, but do not explicitly state a count of distinct plants. A Bengaluru address appears on some directory listings (https://www.indiamart.com/vinyasinnovative-technologies/aboutus.html); the operational manufacturing site is Mysuru. No clearly confirmed second standalone plant found in searched sources.

### Capacity (Production area, SMT lines, etc.)
- **CONFIRMED — Over 1,50,000 sq. ft. (~150,000 sq ft) of production area** (https://www.vinyasit.com/ and https://www.vinyasit.com/)
- **CONFIRMED — PCB assembly lines supporting both SMT and PTH/THT operations; handles multi-layer, flexible and rigid PCBs**, with low/medium/high mix and volume production (https://www.vinyasit.com/ems-capabilities/)
- **CONFIRMED — Additional infrastructure: dedicated NPI/prototype production line, box-build & system integration, and in-house ESS & HASS testing** (https://www.vinyasit.com/ems-capabilities/)
- **CONFIRMED (historical capacity data point) — Manufactured 10,000 energy meters per day (2012)**, cited as evidence of high-volume capability (https://www.vinyasit.com/)
- **NOT DISCLOSED / UNVERIFIED — Exact number of SMT lines and THT lines.** Company sites describe "high-speed SMT lines" but do not give a numeric count. Post-IPO (late 2023) the company reportedly invested in new SMT lines, but specific counts were not found in searched sources (https://www.vinyasit.com/ems-capabilities/)

### Certifications & Approvals
- **CONFIRMED — ISO 9001:2015** (Quality Management) (https://www.vinyasit.com/quality/)
- **CONFIRMED — AS 9100-D** (Aerospace & Defence QMS) (https://www.vinyasit.com/quality/ and https://www.vinyasit.com/aerospace-defence/)
- **CONFIRMED — ISO 13485:2016** (Medical Devices QMS) (https://www.vinyasit.com/quality/)
- **CONFIRMED — NADCAP** (National Aerospace and Defense Contractors Accreditation Program) listed on the Quality page (https://www.vinyasit.com/quality/)
- **CONFIRMED — Defence Industry License** (https://www.vinyasit.com/quality/ and https://www.vinyasit.com/aerospace-defence/)
- **CONFIRMED — IPC/J-STD process standards followed:** IPC-J-STD-003B, IPC-CM-770, IPC-HDBK-001, J-STD-001E, IPC-A-610D (Classes 1/2/3), IPC-WHMA-A-620A (Classes 1/2/3) (https://www.vinyasit.com/quality/)
- **REPORTED (third-party, not on company Quality page) — IATF certification** mentioned in a trade/news profile; treat as secondary until confirmed on company docs (https://www.bitget.com/stock/nse-vinyas/what-is)
- **NOT FOUND / UNVERIFIED — NABL accreditation.** No NABL accreditation was found on the company's Quality page or in searched sources. Cannot confirm.
- **NOT FOUND / UNVERIFIED — ISO 14001 (environmental).** No ISO 14001 certification mentioned on the company Quality page or other sources searched. Cannot confirm.
- **CONFIRMED (general) — Positioned as a strategic supplier to Indian defense PSUs / aerospace & defense supply chains**, with offset-procedure compliance; however, **specific named defense-PSU approvals were not found** in the searched sources (https://www.vinyasit.com/aerospace-defence/ and https://www.indiamanufacturingreview.com/manufacturing/news/vinyas-innovative-technologies-manufacturing-highquality-electronics-for-strategic-sectors-nwid-9139.html)

### Notes on sourcing
- Most facility/capacity facts come from the company's own site (vinyasit.com), which is authoritative for self-disclosure but not independently audited.
- The FY2024-25 NSE SME annual report (https://archives.nseindia.com/annual_reports/SME_AR_28246_VINYAS_2024_2025_A_13318486_03092025110111.pdf) likely contains audited facility/capacity and any new-unit details, but it exceeded the fetch size limit and could not be parsed here — recommended for verifying exact SMT line counts, ISO 14001/NABL status, and any second plant.
- Directory listings (IndiaMART/Justdial) show inconsistent data (e.g., a Bengaluru address, low employee counts) and should be treated as low-reliability.

**Bottom line:** Confirmed are a single ~150,000 sq ft Mysuru manufacturing facility (inaugurated 2014) with an EHTP export unit and a dedicated Class III line; certifications confirmed are ISO 9001:2015, AS 9100-D, ISO 13485:2016, NADCAP, and a Defence Industry License (plus IPC/J-STD process compliance). NABL and ISO 14001 could not be verified; IATF is reported only by a third party; exact SMT line counts are not publicly disclosed in the sources reviewed.
